studyName: Genetics and Functional Studies of Autosomal Recessive Neurological Disorders

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table contains self-reported race, age at enrollment, age at disease onset, status of subject for an intellectual disability, severity status for disability, and statuses for generalized epilepsy, skeletal anomalies, growth retardation, speech delay, and parental consanguinity.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for Intellectual Disability

variables.name: Behavioral_anomalies
variables.description: Behavioral deficits in subject with intellectual disability phenotype

variables.name: Generalized_Epilepsy
variables.description: Generalized epilepsy in subject with the intellectual disability phenotype

variables.name: Growth_retardation
variables.description: Global growth anomalies in subject with intellectual disability phenotype

variables.name: Head_Shape
variables.description: Microcephaly in subject with the intellectual disability phenotype

variables.name: Parents_Consangunity
variables.description: Parent consanguinity of  subject with intellectual disability phenotype

variables.name: Skeletal_anomalies
variables.description: Skeletal defects in subject with the intellectual disability phenotype

variables.name: Speech_delay
variables.description: Speech and language impairment in subject with intellectual disability phenotype

tables.description: This sample attributes data table contains body site, analyte type, tumor status, histological type, and collection age.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

tables.description: This subject consent data table contains subject IDs, consent group information, and biological sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs and pooled sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
